Statement_Summary: APPSolve scope covers configuration design / system config / data migration / testing / training / cutover / hypercare
Risk_If_Invalid: Scope creep into operations and business process management
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: OUM delivery methodology: Mobilize > Scope and Design > Prototype > Validate > Deploy > Evolve
Risk_If_Invalid: Delivery approach misalignment; milestone dispute
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Modules in scope confirmed by signed Oracle BOM; additions are CRs
Risk_If_Invalid: Scope creep from post-SOW module additions
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm BOM before SOW execution

Statement_Summary: Oracle ERP delivered as SaaS; APPSolve configures; Oracle operates; 1 Production + 1 non-production
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes infrastructure responsibility
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: ERP is configuration-based; custom code (APEX/PaaS/Groovy beyond standard config) is separately scoped
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects custom development in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Oracle quarterly SaaS updates managed by Oracle; APPSolve provides implementation services only; update management post-go-live is client responsibility unless AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage quarterly updates post-go-live without AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge SaaS update management responsibility

Statement_Summary: COA design must be signed off by client before ledger configuration commences; delays cascade to all dependent config
Risk_If_Invalid: All downstream config stalls if COA is delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Approve COA design before ledger config starts

Statement_Summary: Legal entity structure confirmed and stable before LE configuration commences; changes after config constitutes rework
Risk_If_Invalid: Legal entity restructure mid-project causes significant rework cost
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm legal entity structure before LE config

Statement_Summary: One primary ledger per legal entity; secondary ledgers assessed as scope addition
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects multiple secondary ledgers (IFRS/local GAAP) in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm secondary ledger requirement before design

Statement_Summary: Client defines COA structure: segment names / lengths / value set definitions / code combination values; APPSolve configures from approved design
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve designs COA from scratch causing ownership and accuracy dispute
Customer_Action_Required: Provide complete approved COA design

Statement_Summary: Default COA includes up to 6 segments; additional segments assessed per complexity
Risk_If_Invalid: COA complexity underestimated; SV rules and reporting more complex than estimated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm COA segment count before configuration

Statement_Summary: Client provides financial calendar (year-end month / period type / year start) before ledger config commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Calendar config blocked without client input
Customer_Action_Required: Provide confirmed accounting calendar

Statement_Summary: Functional currency confirmed per LE before ledger config; SA operations default ZAR
Risk_If_Invalid: Multi-currency setup delayed or wrong currency configured
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm functional currency per legal entity

Statement_Summary: Standard intercompany accounting included in GL base scope where client has multiple LEs; complex netting / multilateral / consolidation assessed separately
Risk_If_Invalid: Complex intercompany scope absorbed into base pricing
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm intercompany complexity in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: One standard journal approval workflow per Oracle ERP instance; additional workflows per BU/category/ledger are CRs
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects separate approval workflow per business unit or journal type
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: FRS reports for standard financial statement pack (IS/BS/TB/CF) at agreed count; additional reports are CRs
Risk_If_Invalid: Report count underestimated; ad hoc report requests treated as in-scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm FRS report list in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Client provides supplier master in FBDI template: legal name / reg no / tax reg / bank details / payment terms / address
Risk_If_Invalid: Supplier load delayed or data quality failures
Customer_Action_Required: Provide supplier data in FBDI template by data migration milestone

Statement_Summary: Automated invoice capture (OCR / IDP / e-invoicing / Ariba / Tungsten) is excluded from base AP scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects OCR invoice capture as standard
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge manual invoice entry as default; raise separately if OCR required

Statement_Summary: Three-way match (PO/GRN/Invoice) is default for PO-based invoices; two-way match where Oracle Receiving not in scope; confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: Incorrect matching approach configured; AP validation failures at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm matching approach in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Non-PO invoices via Oracle AP direct entry; one standard non-PO invoice approval workflow included
Risk_If_Invalid: Multiple non-PO approval workflows per BU expected by client
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: EFT payments via standard South African EFT format; bank format confirmed with client's bank before config; BeBanking H2H where in scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Bank format incompatible; payment file rejected by bank at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm bank payment format with client's bank before config

Statement_Summary: Payment terms configured per client-provided list; APPSolve does not define payment terms policy
Risk_If_Invalid: Payment terms incomplete or not aligned to contracts
Customer_Action_Required: Provide complete approved payment terms list

Statement_Summary: Oracle Supplier Portal (supplier self-service for invoice submission / PO status / profile update) is excluded; requires separate licence and OIC integration
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects supplier portal in base AP scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge exclusion; raise separately if required

Statement_Summary: Client provides customer master in FBDI template: legal name / account no / address / payment terms / credit limit / tax details
Risk_If_Invalid: Customer load delayed or data quality failures
Customer_Action_Required: Provide customer data in FBDI template by migration milestone

Statement_Summary: AR transaction types and transaction sources configured per agreed billing processes; client confirms required types in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: AR transaction type library incomplete; billing fails at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm transaction type requirements in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Auto-invoicing from Oracle module (PPM/Order Mgmt) in same implementation is in AR base scope; auto-invoicing from non-Oracle source requires OIC integration
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects non-Oracle source auto-invoicing in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm invoice source systems in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Standard auto-cash rules configured for EFT receipts; complex cash application (Fx / partial payments with deductions / lockbox) assessed per complexity
Risk_If_Invalid: Cash application failures at go-live due to unscoped complexity
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm cash application requirements in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Oracle AR Collections Management (dunning / collection strategies / aged debt reporting) configured as part of standard AR; client's credit control team manages collections post-go-live
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to run collections management
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Client provides complete asset category list with depreciation rules before FA configuration commences
Risk_If_Invalid: FA configuration blocked without asset category data
Customer_Action_Required: Provide asset category list and depreciation rules before config

Statement_Summary: Opening balance migration: original cost / accumulated depreciation / NBV / remaining useful life; full historical run history excluded; legacy system retained as historical record
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects full historical depreciation history migrated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm opening balance migration approach in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Monthly depreciation run is batch process; client's finance team runs depreciation post-go-live; APPSolve configures process
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to run depreciation batches post-go-live
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: One standard capex approval workflow configured per authority matrix provided by client
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects multiple parallel capex approval workflows
Customer_Action_Required: Provide authority matrix before workflow config

Statement_Summary: Tax asset book assessed per engagement; SA tax depreciation (Section 11/12 SITA / accelerated / SBC) may require separate tracking from accounting book
Risk_If_Invalid: Tax depreciation requirements not identified until late in project
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm whether tax book is required in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Oracle CM configured for bank reconciliation; manual and automated (electronic bank statement import) both supported
Risk_If_Invalid: Bank reconciliation approach misaligned; manual work required post-go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm bank reconciliation approach in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Bank statement import format confirmed with client's bank (ISO 20022 camt.053 or SWIFT MT940 standard); proprietary format requires conversion assessment; BeBanking MT940 where in scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Bank format incompatible; bank statement import fails at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm bank statement format with client's bank

Statement_Summary: Cash positioning configured as standard Oracle CM feature; accuracy depends on completeness of AP payment schedules and AR expected receipts
Risk_If_Invalid: Cash forecast inaccurate due to incomplete AP/AR data
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: BeBanking H2H MT940 inbound integration to Oracle CM is OIC scope (OIC pack); Oracle CM bank account and reconciliation rules are ERP Financials scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Scope boundary confusion between OIC and ERP finance team
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm BeBanking scope before config

Statement_Summary: Procurement modules confirmed by BOM: standard scope includes Self-Service Procurement / Oracle Purchasing / Oracle Receiving; Sourcing and Contracts where licensed
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Sourcing / Contracts in scope without BOM line item
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm Oracle BOM procurement line items

Statement_Summary: Requisition-to-PO flow: purchase requisition > approval > PO > acknowledgement > goods receipt > three-way match to AP; design agreed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: Incomplete Procure-to-Pay process design; failures at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm Procure-to-Pay flow in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: One approval hierarchy per workflow type (Requisition / PO) using AME; default position-based routing; authority matrix provided by client
Risk_If_Invalid: Approval routing incorrect; unauthorised approvals at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Provide complete approved authority matrix before workflow config

Statement_Summary: Hosted item catalogues configured where client provides item data in required template; punchout catalogues require separately scoped OIC integration
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects punchout to supplier catalogues in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm catalogue requirements and provide item data

Statement_Summary: Oracle Procurement Contracts configured per confirmed templates and terms; client's legal team responsible for contract content
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to draft legal contract terms
Customer_Action_Required: Legal team to provide contract content before template config

Statement_Summary: Oracle Receiving in standard Procurement scope for PO-based purchasing; goods receipt workflow confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: Goods receipt process undefined; three-way match fails
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm receiving workflow in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Standard OTBI procurement reports at agreed count; Oracle Procurement Analytics (OAX) separately licensed and excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects advanced spend analytics in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm procurement report requirements

Statement_Summary: PPM modules confirmed by BOM; standard scope: Project Costing; Project Billing added where licensed
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Oracle Project Billing without separate BOM line item
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm PPM BOM line items

Statement_Summary: Client provides project classification structure (project types / statuses / categories) and templates before PPM config commences
Risk_If_Invalid: PPM config blocked without project structure definition
Customer_Action_Required: Provide project type and template definitions

Statement_Summary: Project costing via Oracle-standard cost collection: labour (OTL or manual timecard) / non-labour (AP invoice distribution) / expense (Oracle Expenses where in scope); design confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: Cost collection method incompatible with client's operational model
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm costing collection methods in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Budget type structure and approval workflow configured per Scope and Design; client provides project budget templates before budget config commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Budget config delayed; project financial reporting fails
Customer_Action_Required: Provide project budget templates and approval rules

Statement_Summary: Oracle Project Billing methods: T&M / fixed-price milestones / cost-plus; billing method per project type confirmed in Scope and Design; client confirms billing events / cycles / revenue recognition rules
Risk_If_Invalid: Revenue recognition incorrectly configured; billing disputes
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm billing method and revenue recognition rules in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Oracle PPM to Oracle Financials (GL/AP) is Oracle-native via subledger accounting; not a separately scoped OIC integration; SLA rules configured in ERP scope
Risk_If_Invalid: OIC team scopes a PPM-GL integration that is actually Oracle-native
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Standard OTBI PPM reports at agreed count; Oracle Analytics Cloud requires separate licence and is excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Oracle Analytics Cloud dashboards in base PPM scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm PPM reporting requirements

Statement_Summary: OTBI reporting security configured and agreed custom OTBI reports delivered; count confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: OTBI report count underestimated; ad hoc requests treated as in-scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm OTBI report list in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: FRS financial statement pack at agreed count (IS/BS/TB/CF); additional FRS reports are CRs
Risk_If_Invalid: FRS report count underestimated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm FRS report list in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Smart View enabled and awareness training provided; client builds own Excel models; APPSolve does not build Excel reporting models
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to build Smart View Excel models
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge client responsibility for Smart View Excel model development

Statement_Summary: Oracle Analytics Cloud (OAX/FAW) is separately licensed and excluded; OTBI / FRS / Smart View are included in ERP licence
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects OAX in base ERP scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge OAX exclusion; raise separately if required

Statement_Summary: Oracle ERP captures VAT data; SARS eFiling submission is excluded and is client's tax team responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects automatic SARS eFiling from Oracle ERP
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge eFiling exclusion; client tax team to submit VAT201

Statement_Summary: Data migration via Oracle FBDI; APPSolve provides templates; client populates from source; APPSolve loads and validates
Risk_If_Invalid: FBDI data quality failures at load; migration delays
Customer_Action_Required: Populate FBDI templates per APPSolve specifications

Statement_Summary: Standard migrated data objects: COA value sets / suppliers / customers / open AP invoices / open AR invoices / fixed asset register / projects and budgets / open POs (subject to BOM and scope confirmation)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects additional non-standard data objects migrated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm migration object list in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Historical transaction data not migrated; opening balances only as at cut-over date; legacy system is system of record for history
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects historical journals / paid invoices / closed AR in Oracle ERP
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge historical data exclusion; confirm legacy system retention plan

Statement_Summary: Data extraction from source (legacy) system is client's responsibility; APPSolve provides FBDI templates and format specifications only
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to extract data from legacy system
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate legacy system extraction resources

Statement_Summary: Data quality and cleansing before provision to APPSolve: de-duplicated / complete / accurate / correctly coded; APPSolve validates load correctness not business accuracy
Risk_If_Invalid: Data quality failures surface during load; timeline impact
Customer_Action_Required: Cleanse and validate source data before providing to APPSolve

Statement_Summary: Two migration cycles standard: Mock Migration (non-prod) + Final Migration (production); third mock is scope addition
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects three or more mock migrations in base scope; migration timeline underestimated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm migration cycle count in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Tax registration data (VAT / income tax / customs / PAYE) provided by client before LE and tax configuration commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Tax configuration blocked; legal entity setup delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Provide all tax registration information before config

Statement_Summary: All ERP integrations to third-party systems governed by OIC_ASSUMPTIONS_V1.md; this section covers ERP-specific integration scope boundaries
Risk_If_Invalid: Integration scope boundary dispute between ERP and OIC teams
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Oracle ERP to Oracle HCM (same project) uses Oracle-native APIs; not a separately scoped OIC integration; included in combined HCM+ERP scope
Risk_If_Invalid: OIC team scopes a Fusion-to-Fusion integration that is Oracle-native
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Inter-module Oracle ERP integration (AP/GL subledger / Procurement/AP / PPM/GL) uses Oracle-native architecture; not OIC integrations
Risk_If_Invalid: OIC integration effort wasted on Oracle-native flows
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Bank connectivity (EFT / bank statement import) via BeBanking H2H where in scope or direct bank file format integration; each payment format = one OIC integration (OIC-SCP-002)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects all bank integrations in one OIC integration fee
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm bank connectivity approach and format count in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: SARS eFiling integration excluded; Oracle ERP does not integrate natively with SARS eFiling; client tax team responsible for VAT201/PAYE/provisional tax submission
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects automated SARS eFiling from Oracle ERP
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge eFiling exclusion

Statement_Summary: APPSolve designs Oracle ERP security model: job roles / data security policies / duty roles / abstract roles; Oracle-delivered roles used as starting point; custom roles only where Oracle roles insufficient
Risk_If_Invalid: Security model design scope underestimated
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Custom job role count confirmed in Scope and Design; standard archetypes: Financial Analyst / AP Invoice Supervisor / AR Billing Specialist / Cash Manager / Procurement Manager / Procurement Agent / Project Manager
Risk_If_Invalid: Custom security role count underestimated; security testing effort exceeds estimate
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm security access requirements per role in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Data security policies (row-level: ledger / BU / LE / cost centre restriction) designed in Scope and Design; complex data security (cross-entity partial access) assessed per complexity
Risk_If_Invalid: Complex data security requirements not identified early; design rework
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm data security requirements in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Formal SoD analysis using ACG / Fastpath or equivalent excluded from base scope; APPSolve designs roles following Oracle SoD guidelines only
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects formal SoD audit as part of ERP implementation
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge SoD exclusion; raise separately if required by audit

Statement_Summary: User provisioning post-go-live (creating / role-assigning / deactivating users) is client IT team's responsibility; APPSolve configures provisioning framework during implementation
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to manage user accounts post-go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate IT resource for ongoing user lifecycle management

Statement_Summary: SSO integration with client's IdP (Azure AD / Okta / Ping / Google) assessed as separately scoped item; Oracle SAML 2.0 SSO is a platform feature; IdP integration requires separate design and config engagement
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects SSO configuration in base ERP scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge SSO assessment as separate scope item

Statement_Summary: All Oracle ERP approval workflows configured using Oracle AME; authority matrix provided by client before workflow config commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Workflow config blocked without authority matrix; go-live delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Provide complete approved authority matrix before workflow config

Statement_Summary: Standard approval workflows in base scope (subject to BOM): manual journal (GL) / non-PO invoice (AP) / purchase requisition (Procurement) / PO (Procurement) / capex (FA) / project budget (PPM); each = one APPSolve config item
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects more workflow types than estimated in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm which workflow types are required in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: One approval hierarchy per workflow type; multiple parallel hierarchies per workflow (per BU / department / ledger) are CRs
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects separate approval chains per business unit for every workflow
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm approval hierarchy structure in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Approval notifications via Oracle standard BPM Worklist notification framework; custom HTML-branded email templates excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects custom-branded approval notification emails
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge standard notification format

Statement_Summary: Delegation rules (absence delegation of approval authority) enabled as Oracle platform feature; users manage own delegation rules post-go-live
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage approval delegation rules for all users
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Approval limits configured per client's authority matrix; monetary threshold changes post-config are low-effort updates not CRs unless structural workflow redesign required
Risk_If_Invalid: Every approval limit change treated as a CR regardless of impact
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm authority matrix is approved and stable before workflow config

Statement_Summary: Three testing cycles: CRP1 (Design Validation) / CRP2 (End-to-End Validation) / UAT (User Acceptance); aligned to OUM testing framework
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes testing cycle structure mid-project
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: CRP1: APPSolve demonstrates configured system against Scope and Design; client functional team validates design; not a UAT event
Risk_If_Invalid: Client tries to conduct UAT in CRP1 before system is fully configured
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: CRP2: end-to-end process testing across all ERP modules; client executes scenarios; APPSolve resolves configuration defects
Risk_If_Invalid: Client treats business process deficiencies as configuration defects
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: UAT: client user community validates system against requirements; APPSolve supports but does not execute; UAT sign-off is client's responsibility and prerequisite for cutover
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to execute UAT and sign off on behalf of client
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate UAT testing resources and sign-off authority

Statement_Summary: Test data provision for all cycles is client's responsibility; must cover standard and exception scenarios for all modules
Risk_If_Invalid: Test data incomplete; testing cycles delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Prepare and provide test data by testing milestone

Statement_Summary: Defect classification: build defects resolved by APPSolve; data / process / design defects resolved by client; formal defect log maintained throughout
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes defect ownership; APPSolve absorbs client data issues
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge defect classification framework

Statement_Summary: APPSolve delivers TTT for agreed ERP user populations: GL/Reporting / AP / AR / Cash Management / Procurement / PPM (per BOM modules)
Risk_If_Invalid: Training population scope underestimated
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm training populations in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: End-user training delivery is client's responsibility; APPSolve provides TTT and configured-system training materials; client's trained trainers deliver to end users
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to train all end users directly
Customer_Action_Required: Nominate trainers for TTT and plan end-user training delivery

Statement_Summary: Training materials based on client's configured Oracle ERP (client-specific COA / workflows / data / transaction types); not generic Oracle product training
Risk_If_Invalid: Generic training materials delivered that don't reflect client's system
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Oracle University formal training / Oracle certification courses excluded from APPSolve training scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Oracle certification training from APPSolve
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge Oracle University exclusion; client arranges separately

Statement_Summary: One production cutover event; cutover approach (big-bang vs. phased) confirmed in Scope and Design; APPSolve recommends big-bang for interdependent ERP modules
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects phased go-live without understanding interdependency
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm cutover approach in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: APPSolve produces cutover runbook: pre-cutover steps / cutover day steps / go-live confirmation criteria
Risk_If_Invalid: Cutover day uncoordinated; unexpected issues cause go-live failure
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Client implements legacy system transaction freeze for final data extraction; manages internal communication of freeze period
Risk_If_Invalid: Data changed in legacy system during FBDI load invalidating migration data
Customer_Action_Required: Plan and communicate transaction freeze period

Statement_Summary: Go-live decision by client's project sponsor based on APPSolve's written go/no-go recommendation; final decision rests with client
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to make go-live call; sponsor defers decision
Customer_Action_Required: Ensure project sponsor is available and empowered for go-live decision

Statement_Summary: Parallel running excluded from base scope; simultaneous dual-system operation significantly extends effort; assessed as scope addition where required
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects parallel running as standard; effort and timeline impact disputed
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge parallel running exclusion

Statement_Summary: ERP hypercare: 4 weeks / business hours 08:00-17:00 SAST Mon-Fri / defect resolution and stabilisation only; aligned to HCM Base HYP assumptions
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects 24/7 hypercare or extended support period in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Combined ERP + HCM + OIC hypercare is one concurrent 4-week period covering all modules and integrations
Risk_If_Invalid: Client argues OIC and ERP hypercare should each be separate 4-week periods
Customer_Action_Required: None

Statement_Summary: Enhancements during hypercare excluded: new GL accounts / new workflows / new reports / new modules; these are CRs or new project work
Risk_If_Invalid: Client uses hypercare to extend scope; enhancements absorbed into support
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge enhancement exclusion during hypercare

Statement_Summary: First month-end close after go-live supported during hypercare; if hypercare ends before first month-end close APPSolve extends at standard hypercare daily rate
Risk_If_Invalid: First month-end close attempt without APPSolve support; high risk failure
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm go-live date relative to month-end close date

Statement_Summary: Client appoints ERP project sponsor with authority to approve scope / resolve design escalations / authorise go-live / drive change management
Risk_If_Invalid: No empowered sponsor; design decisions stall; go-live delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Nominate and confirm project sponsor before project starts

Statement_Summary: Client appoints functional leads per module: Finance Lead (GL/AP/AR/FA/CM) / Procurement Lead / PPM Lead; available full project duration; attend workshops and CRP; make domain decisions and sign off milestones
Risk_If_Invalid: Functional team unavailable for workshops; design quality poor; delays
Customer_Action_Required: Nominate functional leads before Mobilize phase

Statement_Summary: COA design is the client's most critical deliverable in Scope and Design; client provides or approves COA before ledger config; APPSolve facilitates COA workshops
Risk_If_Invalid: COA delayed; all downstream config stalls
Customer_Action_Required: Prioritise COA design as top deliverable in Scope and Design phase

Statement_Summary: Client extracts / cleanses / provides all migration data in FBDI templates by project plan milestone dates; suppliers / customers / FA / open AP / open AR / open POs / projects all require client prep
Risk_If_Invalid: Data provided late; migration milestones missed; go-live delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate data migration resource and agree data provision dates in project plan

Statement_Summary: Client cleanses source data before provision: removes duplicates / populates mandatory fields / corrects GL codes / updates inactive records / confirms bank account details
Risk_If_Invalid: Data quality failures surface during FBDI load; rework cycles
Customer_Action_Required: Complete data cleansing before providing FBDI templates to APPSolve

Statement_Summary: Client's functional team makes all business process decisions in Scope and Design: transaction types / approval routing / payment terms / COA structure / billing method; APPSolve facilitates and documents
Risk_If_Invalid: Business process decisions deferred to APPSolve causing ownership disputes
Customer_Action_Required: Empower functional team to make binding process decisions

Statement_Summary: Client provides complete approved authority matrix for all transaction types requiring approval before workflow config commences; authorised by CFO/CEO/Finance Director
Risk_If_Invalid: Approval workflow configured on unapproved authority matrix; rework required after governance sign-off
Customer_Action_Required: Obtain CFO/Finance Director approval for authority matrix before workflow config

Statement_Summary: Client's tax team provides all tax configuration requirements: SA VAT reg no / VAT rates / tax classifications / withholding tax / industry-specific treatment; APPSolve configures from client requirements not tax advice
Risk_If_Invalid: Tax configuration incorrect due to incomplete requirements; statutory compliance risk
Customer_Action_Required: Tax team to provide complete tax requirements before LE and tax config

Statement_Summary: Client's finance and tax team responsible for statutory and regulatory compliance: Companies Act / SARS VAT-PAYE / IFRS or GAAP / industry-specific; APPSolve configures per client requirements only
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held liable for statutory non-compliance
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge APPSolve provides configuration services not compliance advice

Statement_Summary: Client's functional team and user community participate in CRP1 / CRP2 / UAT; provides test data / UAT testers / executes scenarios / logs defects / provides written sign-off at each milestone
Risk_If_Invalid: Testing cycles delayed due to unavailable client testers; UAT fails
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate testing resources and confirm sign-off authorities before CRP

Statement_Summary: Client responsible for end-user training delivery / scheduling / communication / adoption measurement; APPSolve delivers TTT only
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to deliver end-user training to all employees
Customer_Action_Required: Plan end-user training programme and allocate trainers

Statement_Summary: Internal change management (communicating ERP project / managing legacy system transition / adoption challenges) is client leadership and HR responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Change management omitted; low adoption post-go-live; system underutilised
Customer_Action_Required: Appoint change management lead and plan communications programme

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion Global Payroll excluded; SA payroll managed by third-party provider (PaySpace/Sage 300/VIP) integrated via OIC
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Oracle Payroll native in ERP scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge payroll exclusion

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion Expenses excluded from base ERP scope unless in BOM; separately licensed and separately scoped where required
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects expense claims in base ERP scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge Expenses exclusion; raise if required

Statement_Summary: SARS eFiling integration excluded; VAT201 / provisional tax / PAYE returns are client tax team responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects automated SARS submission from Oracle ERP
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge eFiling exclusion

Statement_Summary: Formal SoD analysis and remediation using ACG/Fastpath or equivalent excluded; APPSolve applies Oracle SoD guidelines in role design
Risk_If_Invalid: Client or auditor requires formal SoD conflict report; not scoped
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge SoD tool exclusion; raise separately if auditor requires it

Statement_Summary: Oracle Supplier Portal (supplier self-service) excluded; requires separate Oracle licence and OIC integration
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects supplier-facing portal in base scope
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge Supplier Portal exclusion

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion Risk Management (Advanced Controls) separately licensed and excluded from base ERP scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects financial controls monitoring in base scope

Statement_Summary: Legacy ERP system decommissioning excluded; client's responsibility post go-live

Customer_Action_Required: Plan legacy system decommissioning independently

Statement_Summary: Parallel running excluded from base scope; see ERP-CUT-005

Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge parallel running exclusion

Statement_Summary: Business process redesign excluded; APPSolve configures Oracle ERP to support agreed future-state processes

Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge BPR exclusion; engage separately if required

Statement_Summary: Invoice OCR and intelligent document capture excluded from base AP scope; standard AP is manual invoice entry

Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge OCR exclusion; raise separately if required

Statement_Summary: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud for complex IFRS 15/ASC 606 scenarios separately licensed; standard Oracle AR revenue recognition included; complex scenarios require assessment

Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge complex revenue recognition exclusion; raise if required

Statement_Summary: Audit deliverables and CAAT reports excluded; standard OTBI reports available for audit support; bespoke audit deliverables not in implementation scope
